title: `--tls-verify` parsing problem
body:
### Issue Description

The `--tls-verify` option seems to be particularly picky about how its argument is specified. For example:

```bash
$ podman login --tls-verify false registry.example.com
Error: accepts at most 1 arg(s), received 2
```
 
You have to write `--tls-verify=false` for this example to work. This is in contrast to other CLI options which generally accept both space-separated and equal-separated arguments.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Run `podman login --tls-verify false registry.example.com`

### Describe the results you received

Command errors.

### Describe the results you expected

Proceeds to login prompt.

### Additional information

comments.body:
That is because --tls-verify is a bool option and the syntax for bool options is defined like this.

If we change this to a string argument the parser would no longer accept just --tls-verify.
